steps.title: Identify the contrast with sentimentality
steps.content: The passage contrasts Whistler's approach with his contemporaries' sentimental methods. The answer must be the opposite of sentimental.

steps.title: Match the opposite of sentimental
steps.content:
Substitute each choice: Whistler's eye seemed ______ (contrasted with his contemporaries' sentimental approaches).

A) tranquil — "tranquil" means calm or peaceful. This describes mood, not emotional detachment. A sentimental painting can also be tranquil. Doesn't capture the contrast. Eliminate.

B) peculiar — "peculiar" means strange or unusual. The passage contrasts Whistler with sentimentality, not with normality. Oddness is unrelated to the sentimental vs. analytical distinction. Eliminate.

C) nostalgic — "nostalgic" means wistfully longing for the past — a form of sentimentality. This would put Whistler on the same side as his contemporaries, not opposite them. Eliminate.

D) dispassionate — "dispassionate" means without emotion or bias, objective. The passage links Whistler to Walker Evans's "objective documentations of life," confirming this unemotional, analytical quality. Keep.

D is correct.
